targets.$comment: Baselines captured 2026-04-14 on i9-13900KS (Windows Docker Desktop) via --job short against Testcontainers redis:7-alpine. Means are in microseconds at the wire because the Docker bridge adds ~1ms of loopback latency + jitter; the Linux CI runner runs Docker natively and is typically 2-3x faster. Targets apply ~3x headroom over local Windows numbers to absorb runner variance. Revisit after first CI run.

stabilityOverrides.$comment: Docker-bridge RTT to a Testcontainers redis:7-alpine instance dominates each benchmark. Loopback latency on Windows Docker Desktop fluctuates between 200us and 2ms per round-trip, so CoV stays above 20% even at N=20. The measurements are meaningful as orders of magnitude (microseconds vs. the nanosecond paths of Memory/Hybrid) but not precise enough for stable regression detection. All methods listed.
stabilityOverrides.RedisCacheProviderBenchmarks.ExistsAsync: Docker loopback jitter dominates; CoV ~45% at N=20.
stabilityOverrides.RedisCacheProviderBenchmarks.GetAsync_Ca: Docker loopback jitter dominates; CoV ~34% at N=20.
Docker loopback jitter dominates; CoV ~36% at N=20, multimodal.
stabilityOverrides.RedisCacheProviderBenchmarks.GetOrSetAsy: Docker loopback jitter dominates; CoV ~46% at N=20, bimodal.
Factory invocation + Redis write; CoV ~18% at N=20.
stabilityOverrides.RedisCacheProviderBenchmarks.RemoveAsync: Docker loopback jitter dominates; CoV ~49% at N=20.
stabilityOverrides.RedisCacheProviderBenchmarks.RemoveByPat: SCAN + pipelined DEL on 5 keys; CoV ~30% at N=20.
stabilityOverrides.RedisCacheProviderBenchmarks.SetAsync: Docker loopback jitter dominates; CoV ~30% at N=20, bimodal.
stabilityOverrides.RedisCacheProviderBenchmarks.SetWithSlid: Docker loopback jitter dominates; CoV ~41% at N=20.
